LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES IS SUSTAINED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO ALL THREE PLAINTIFFS WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE EITHER DIRECT CLAIMS BY PLAINTIFF 580 SUTTER LLC OR DERIVATIVE CLAIMS WHERE 580 SUTTER LLC IS NAMED AS A NOMINAL DEFENDANT AND REMOVED AS A PLAINTIFF (SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 60093574) FILED BY DEFENDANT MCDONNELL, DUGGAN P JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO STRIKE THE FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFFS CHRISTINE PRENTICE AND AARON PRENTICE IS DENIED. WHILE A LITERAL READING OF THE ORDER OLD REPUBLIC'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT WOULD LIMIT LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLAINTIFF 580 SUTTER LLC, THAT WAS NOT THE COURT'S INTENT. MOREOVER, WERE THE PRENTICES TO REQUEST LEAVE TO AMEND, IT WOULD BE GRANTED GIVEN THE LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AND THE ABSENCE OF ANY DISCERNABLE PREJUDICE TO OLD REPUBLIC. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFFS CHRISTINE PRENTICE AND AARON PRENTICE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY AND A NEGLIGENCE DUTY OF CARE FAIL BECAUSE THE ESCROW CONTRACT EXPLICITLY DISCLAIMS ANY DUTIES IMPOSED ON OLD REPUBLIC EXCEPT AS TO THE SIGNATORIES TO THE CONTRACT. BECAUSE THE PRENTICES HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THERE IS ANY REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THEY CAN ALLEGE A VIABLE BREACH OF CONTRACT OR NEGLIGENCE CLAIM AGAINST OLD REPUBLIC, THEY ARE EXPRESSLY DENIED LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, THE ALLEGATIONS THAT OLD REPUBLIC AIDED AND ABETTED MR. MCDONNELL IN HIS ALLEGED BREACH OF HIS FIDUCIARY DUTY OF DUTY AS TO THE PRENTICES ARE SUFFICIENT TO WITHSTAND A DEMURRER. (CACI 3610 AND 4100). WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Continued To Mar-29-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Laura L. Gildengorin of the law offices of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (415.421.3400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Old Republic

No other appearance is made in this matter

TDEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to strike the fifth, sixth and seventh causes of actions in the first amended complaint alleged by plaintiffs Christine Prentice and Aaron Prentice is DENIED. While a literal reading of the order Old Republic's demurrer to the complaint would limit leave to amend to plaintiff 580 Sutter LLC, that was not the court's intent. Moreover, were the Prentices to request leave to amend, it would be granted given the liberal policy in favor of allowing amendments to pleadings and the absence of any discernable prejudice to Old Republic. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Laura L. Gildengorin of the law offices of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (415.421.3400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Old Republic

No other appearance is made in this matter

TDEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the fifth, sixth and seventh causes of actions in the first amended complaint alleged by plaintiffs Christine Prentice and Aaron Prentice is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for breach of contract and the seventh cause of action for negligence and overruled as to the sixth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. The allegations of third party beneficiary and a negligence duty of care fail because the escrow contract explicitly disclaims any duties imposed on Old Republic except as to the signatories to the contract. Because the Prentices have not shown that there is any reasonable possibility that they can allege a viable breach of contract or negligence claim against Old Republic, they are expressly denied leave to amend as to the fifth and seventh causes of action. Liberally construed, the allegations that Old Republic aided and abetted Mr. McDonnell in his alleged breach of his fiduciary duty of duty as to the Prentices are sufficient to withstand a demurrer. (CACI 3610 and 4100). Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-29-2016 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Lowenthal of Steyer Lowenthal LLP telephone number 415-421-3400 for Defendant Old Republic Title Company.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Old Republic Title Company's demurrer to the fifth, sixth and seventh causes is sustained as to all causes of action as to all three plaintiffs with leave to amend to allege either direct claims by plaintiff 580 Sutter LLC or derivative claims where 580 Sutter LLC is named as a nominal defendant and removed as a plaintiff. Plaintiffs Christine Prentice and Aaron Prentice lack standing to assert any of the three claims on their own behalf. The complaint is uncertain whether claims on behalf of 580 Sutter LLC are alleged as direct or derivative claims. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
